thesis: Alert fatigue is a triage problem, not a detection problem.

artifacts.title: Daily seasonality + attack overlay (wheeler)
artifacts.insight: Alert volume swings on a strong daily cycle; a seasonally-aware detector flags the off-rhythm spikes and catches 50% of attack-hours — but precision stays at 8%, because one noisy signature dominates the residual. Seasonality is necessary, not sufficient: the signal has to move from volume to behavior.
artifacts.data_quality_note: Intended source LANL auth logs is now form-gated; this uses the AIT-ADS alert stream with attack windows as ground truth (attack windows are coarse, not dispositions).

artifacts.title: Per-entity baselining: real signal, too little history
artifacts.insight: The biggest per-host deviations are attack-linked (mean z 136 vs 1.8 on normal days), but baselining barely out-ranks raw volume (AUC 0.61 vs 0.61). With ~5 days of history the per-entity baseline is too short to trust — the data, not the method, is the bottleneck.
artifacts.data_quality_note: LANL auth logs (the ideal source) are now form-gated; AIT-ADS host-days substitute.
artifacts.fit_warning: Baseline AUC (~0.61) barely beats raw volume because the testbed gives only ~5 days of per-host history and labels are coarse attack-windows, not analyst dispositions. Behavioral baselining is promising but unprovable on public data — the case for a labeled-disposition dataset.

artifacts.title: 2,655,821 alerts → 1,151 patterns → 22 archetypes
artifacts.insight: 2,655,821 raw alerts are really 1,151 recurring (signature, host) patterns — a 99.96% collapse. One pattern alone (“Web server 400 error code.”) is 59% of all alerts. Deduplication is the highest-leverage triage lever, before any model runs.
artifacts.data_quality_note: Labels are scenario attack-time windows, not analyst dispositions: a benign alert inside an attack window still counts as attack-time. Severity here is a proxy.

artifacts.title: 39 features → 11 components (90% variance)
artifacts.insight: 39 flow features collapse into 11 orthogonal components capturing 90% of the variance — the alert space is far lower-dimensional than it looks.
artifacts.data_quality_note: PCA, not classical latent-factor analysis; a preprocessing/structure view that feeds clustering and regression rather than a standalone insight.

artifacts.title: 15 features carry 90% of the signal (AUC 0.983)
artifacts.insight: A handful of features — led by sttl and smean — carry the weight: 15 of 39 features explain 90% of the model's discrimination (AUC 0.983). The model is accurate AND can say why — but that accuracy is partly an artifact, and it predicts an attack label, not an analyst's verdict.
artifacts.fit_warning: AUC this high is partly a UNSW-NB15 separability artifact (e.g. near-separable sttl) and will not transfer to production triage. The target is an attack label, a PROXY for the analyst disposition that no public dataset provides.

artifacts.title: Breach risk is concentrated: 5 patches halve it
artifacts.insight: This 60-CVE asset expects 7.6 CVEs to be exploited in 30 days, and the risk is concentrated: patching the 5 KEV/EPSS-flagged CVEs first halves that exposure, while random patching barely moves it. Prioritization, not patch volume, is the lever.
artifacts.fit_warning: Monte Carlo is a forced fit on telemetry; it is honest only on this EPSS/CVE risk layer. The asset portfolio is constructed (no public asset-inventory data), so this demonstrates the prioritization method, not a measured breach rate.

artifacts.title: Urgency in the language (AUC 0.99)
artifacts.insight: Only 2.5% of cyber-news tweets are genuinely high-risk, yet a simple urgency classifier finds them at AUC 0.99. The words that fire — warns, warning, confirms — are the same urgency cues that wear analysts down: the human cost is real and it is in the language.
artifacts.fit_warning: Emotional-tone sentiment is the wrong frame; this is urgency/risk text CLASSIFICATION, which is legitimate. It runs on tweets, not SOC alerts, so it corroborates the human cost of alert fatigue rather than carrying the triage argument.
